title: JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications
summary: A modern JavaScript course covering the ECMAScript value and object models, functions, modules, DOM events, asynchronous work, HTTP, browser state, security, testing and application structure.

audience.target_capability: Independently design, implement, test and debug a modular browser application that manipulates semantic DOM, handles asynchronous data and respects security and accessibility boundaries.

includes: Storage, URL and browser security basics
Automated tests and a modular browser project

authoritative_sources.title: OWASP Cross Site Scripting Prevention Cheat Sheet

runtime.standard: Current ECMAScript implemented by the declared evergreen browser matrix
runtime.execution_environment: Sandboxed browser origin with mocked network services and deterministic fixtures
notes: Browser and ECMAScript behavior are identified separately.
Experimental proposals are excluded until standardized and supported by the target matrix.

modules.title: JavaScript execution and tools
modules.description: Run scripts and modules while understanding the browser execution environment.

skills.title: Scripts and console
skills.description: Load scripts intentionally and use the console for bounded experiments rather than application output.
outcomes: Attach and run a script, then explain source order and observable output.

skills.title: Module loading baseline
skills.description: Load a module with deferred execution, strict semantics and explicit imports.
outcomes: Create and load a minimal two-module browser program.

skills.title: Sources, console and debugger
skills.description: Set breakpoints, inspect scope and distinguish logged values from live object views.
outcomes: Use developer tools to locate and explain a state-transition defect.

skills.title: Call stack and event loop model
skills.description: Distinguish synchronous stack execution, tasks and microtasks at an introductory level.
outcomes: Predict logged output order for synchronous calls, promises and timer tasks.

modules.title: Values, types and expressions
modules.description: Use JavaScript's value model while controlling coercion and numeric edge cases.

skills.title: Primitive values and typeof
skills.description: Use undefined, null, Boolean, number, bigint, string and symbol with accurate type expectations.
outcomes: Classify primitive and object values and explain null and undefined use cases.

skills.title: let, const and binding
skills.description: Use block-scoped declarations and distinguish binding reassignment from object mutation.
outcomes: Trace bindings through blocks and choose const unless reassignment is intended.

skills.title: Equality, truthiness and coercion
skills.description: Prefer explicit conversion and strict equality while understanding common coercion rules.
outcomes: Predict and repair equality, concatenation and truthiness defects.

skills.title: Numbers, NaN and precision
skills.description: Handle finite checks, safe integers, floating approximation and bigint boundaries.
outcomes: Implement and test numeric validation without relying on misleading NaN comparisons.

modules.title: Control flow and functions
modules.description: Design clear decisions, loops and callable interfaces with correct scope.

skills.title: Conditions and switch
skills.description: Write complete branches with deliberate truth tests and lexical scoping.
outcomes: Implement and test boundary-complete conditional logic.

skills.title: Loops and iterable traversal
skills.description: Choose for, while, for-of and collection methods while avoiding inappropriate for-in array traversal.
outcomes: Implement and debug counted, sentinel and iterable loops.

skills.title: Function declarations, expressions and arrows
skills.description: Choose callable forms while understanding hoisting, lexical this and expression returns.
outcomes: Implement equivalent callable behavior and explain where arrow semantics differ.

skills.title: Parameters, rest and return contracts
skills.description: Use defaults, destructuring, rest parameters and explicit return behavior without shared mutable defaults.
outcomes: Design and test a function with a clear parameter and failure contract.

modules.title: Arrays, objects, maps and sets
modules.description: Select and transform common mutable and immutable-shaped data structures.

skills.title: Arrays and indexed data
skills.description: Create, index, copy and mutate arrays with explicit bounds and sparse-array awareness.
outcomes: Implement and test array traversal, update and copy operations.

skills.title: Array transformation methods
skills.description: Use map, filter, find, some, every and reduce with callbacks whose effects are clear.
outcomes: Construct and explain a transformation pipeline and compare it with an explicit loop.

skills.title: Object records and property access
skills.description: Create record-like objects, use computed properties and distinguish missing from undefined values.
outcomes: Model and validate a small domain record with property shorthand and safe access.

skills.title: Map, Set and structured copying
skills.description: Choose keyed or unique-value collections and understand spread as a shallow operation.
outcomes: Select and use Array, Object, Map or Set and identify aliasing in a nested copy.

modules.title: Objects, prototypes and classes
modules.description: Reason about property lookup and create maintainable object abstractions.

skills.title: Method calls and this
skills.description: Determine this from call form and avoid detached-method and arrow-method surprises.
outcomes: Trace and repair this binding across direct, method and callback calls.

skills.title: Prototype delegation
skills.description: Trace own and inherited property lookup without treating prototypes as class copies.
outcomes: Inspect and explain a short prototype chain and a shadowed property.

skills.title: Classes and private state
skills.description: Use class syntax, constructors, methods and private fields for justified stateful abstractions.
outcomes: Implement and test a class that preserves an invariant.

skills.title: Composition and inheritance
skills.description: Prefer composed functions or collaborators and use extends only for substitutable behavior.
outcomes: Review and refactor an object design using justified composition or inheritance.

modules.title: Scope, closures and modules
modules.description: Control lifetime and dependency boundaries across functions and files.

skills.title: Lexical scope and temporal dead zone
skills.description: Resolve names through nested blocks and functions without relying on var hoisting.
outcomes: Trace name resolution and repair shadowing or use-before-initialization defects.

skills.title: Closures and lifetime
skills.description: Use retained lexical state for factories and callbacks while avoiding accidental stale or shared state.
outcomes: Implement and trace a closure-based abstraction with independent instances.

skills.title: Imports, exports and module graph
skills.description: Create explicit named or default exports and understand live bindings and cycles conceptually.
outcomes: Organize a feature into modules and diagnose an import or cycle problem.

skills.title: Dependency boundaries
skills.description: Separate domain logic, browser adapters and presentation so core behavior is directly testable.
outcomes: Design and implement a module graph without circular feature dependencies or hidden globals.

modules.title: DOM, events and forms
modules.description: Update semantic documents through safe, accessible event-driven behavior.

skills.title: Query and update DOM
skills.description: Select elements and update text, attributes, classes and properties with correct distinctions.
outcomes: Implement a DOM update without unsafe HTML parsing or unnecessary tree replacement.

skills.title: Create and remove nodes
skills.description: Build document fragments and preserve semantics, ownership and focus during updates.
outcomes: Render a data collection with safe node creation and stable semantic structure.

skills.title: Event propagation and delegation
skills.description: Handle capture, target and bubble phases and delegate stable ancestor events when appropriate.
outcomes: Trace propagation and implement a delegated interaction without duplicate handlers.

skills.title: Forms, validation and accessible state
skills.description: Use form events and constraint APIs while preserving labels, native submission and perceivable errors.
outcomes: Implement client validation as enhancement and verify keyboard focus and error announcement behavior.

modules.title: Promises, async work and HTTP
modules.description: Coordinate asynchronous work and communicate with remote resources robustly.

skills.title: Promises and chains
skills.description: Represent eventual completion and compose fulfillment, rejection and cleanup without floating promises.
outcomes: Trace and implement a promise chain with correctly propagated failures.

skills.title: async and await
skills.description: Write structured asynchronous functions and distinguish sequential from intentional concurrent waits.
outcomes: Refactor a promise workflow using async/await and justify its concurrency behavior.

skills.title: Fetch and HTTP responses
skills.description: Construct requests and handle status, headers, body parsing, network failures and cross-origin constraints.
outcomes: Implement and test a JSON request that distinguishes HTTP failure from network rejection.

skills.title: Cancellation, timeout and stale results
skills.description: Abort obsolete work and prevent older responses from overwriting current interface state.
outcomes: Implement and test cancellation or latest-request-wins behavior for an interactive fetch.

modules.title: Browser state and security
modules.description: Use browser persistence and URLs without creating injection or privacy failures.

skills.title: URL and history state
skills.description: Parse and update URL search and history state without manual unsafe string construction.
outcomes: Implement shareable filter state and handle back-forward navigation.

skills.title: Web storage and serialization
skills.description: Persist small non-sensitive preferences with versioned parsing and quota or corruption handling.
outcomes: Implement resilient local preference storage without treating it as trusted input.

skills.title: DOM injection prevention
skills.description: Keep untrusted text out of executable HTML, URL and event-handler contexts.
outcomes: Identify and repair DOM XSS sinks using safe text or constrained APIs.

skills.title: Client secrets and privacy
skills.description: Recognize that browser-delivered values are observable and minimize third-party data disclosure.
outcomes: Review a client design and remove embedded secrets or unnecessary personal-data persistence.

modules.title: Errors, tests and application project
modules.description: Verify modular browser behavior and deliver an accessible asynchronous application.

skills.title: Errors, throw and recovery boundaries
skills.description: Create meaningful errors, preserve causes and recover only where a user or caller can act.
outcomes: Design and test error behavior across domain, network and presentation modules.

skills.title: Pure and module tests
skills.description: Test deterministic domain behavior and module contracts with isolated fixtures.
outcomes: Create tests covering normal, boundary and failure behavior for non-DOM modules.

skills.title: DOM and asynchronous integration tests
skills.description: Test user-observable state, events, loading, failure and cancellation rather than implementation details.
outcomes: Create deterministic tests for an accessible event and fetch workflow.

skills.title: Modular browser application
skills.description: Integrate semantic HTML, modules, state, DOM, forms, fetch, errors, security and tests.
outcomes: Deliver and explain a keyboard-usable modular browser application with tested loading, empty, success, error and stale-request states.

exit_outcomes: Explain and debug JavaScript values, coercion, functions, closures, prototypes, modules and asynchronous ordering.
Build a modular browser interface using semantic DOM, accessible events and enhanced native forms.
Fetch and validate remote data with explicit loading, failure, cancellation and stale-result behavior.
Prevent client-side injection and secret exposure and verify behavior through unit and browser integration tests.
